headline: SentinelOne's quarterly profit forecast falls short of estimates amid stiff competition
summary: Cybersecurity firm SentinelOne forecast quarterly profit below Wall Street estimates on Thursday, signaling a cautious stance ‌amid intense competition from larger rivals and a finance leadership ‌transition. Shares of the Mountain View, California-based company were down over 2% after the bell. The ​company faces competition from larger rivals such as CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks, as well as from Microsoft, which is bundling security features into its enterprise software.

headline: Pellera Technologies Awarded the Palo Alto Networks 2025 North America Small-Medium Business Partner of the Year
summary: Pellera Technologies, a leader in enterprise IT solutions, is pleased to announce it has received the Palo Alto Networks 2025 Business Partner of the Year for North America in the Small-Medium business category.

headline: TD SYNNEX Awarded the Palo Alto Networks 2025 North American Distributor of the Year for Fourth Year
summary: FREMONT, Calif. & CLEARWATER, Fla., March 12, 2026--TD SYNNEX (NYSE:SNX), a leading global distributor and solutions aggregator for the IT ecosystem, today announced it has received the Palo Alto Networks 2025 North American Distributor of the Year Award.

headline: Zscaler Stock Plunges 34% in Three Months: Time to Exit or Hold Tight?
summary: ZS shares decline 33.7% in three months amid macro pressure, but AI security, Zero Trust growth and strong results point to potential long-term upside.

headline: With Cyberattacks Surging, Does CrowdStrike or Palo Alto Networks Stand Out?
summary: With pro-Iran retaliatory cyberattacks escalating following U.S.-Israeli strikes, CISA leadership in turmoil, and AI agents rapidly expanding corporate attack surfaces, the cybersecurity investment case has never been more urgent. The market is projected to grow from $255 billion to $580 billion by 2031 at a 14.68% CAGR. In a rising tide, platform selection matters. So ... With Cyberattacks Surging, Does CrowdStrike or Palo Alto Networks Stand Out?

headline: Palo Alto Networks Wins Israeli Tender Raising Questions On Valuation And Growth
summary: Palo Alto Networks (NasdaqGS:PANW) has been selected to supply cybersecurity and information security products and services for Israel’s largest network security tender. The project will be delivered through partner Danet Communications, a subsidiary of Accel Solutions Group. The tender covers critical national infrastructure and is structured as a multi-year engagement with recurring revenue potential. Palo Alto Networks focuses on network, cloud, and security operations products, with an...
